---
title: "How to Collect and Analyze Scholarly Metadata (from scratch) with the Crossref REST API for absolute beginners"
format:
  live-revealjs:
    width: 1300 # 1050 def
    height: 900 # 700 def
    code-line-numbers: true
    include-in-header:
    - text: |
         <link rel="stylesheet" href="/css/presentation_styles.css">
         <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/jsoneditor/dist/jsoneditor.min.css">

engine: knitr
webr:
  cell-options:
    caption: "R Code"
  packages:
    - jsonlite
    - httr2
    - listviewer
pyodide:
  cell-options:
    caption: Python Code    
  packages:
    - requests
---

```{r echo=FALSE}
# Setup knitr for handling {webr} and {pyodide} blocks
# TODO: With quarto-dev/quarto-cli#10169, we can implement this in a filter

# We'll handle `include: false` in Lua, always include cell in knitr output
knitr::opts_hooks$set(include = function(options) {
    if (options$engine == "webr" || options$engine == "pyodide") {
        options$include <- TRUE
    }
    options
})

# Passthrough engine for webr
knitr::knit_engines$set(webr = function(options) {
    knitr:::one_string(c(
        "```{webr}",
        options$yaml.code,
        options$code,
        "```"
    ))
})

# Passthrough engine for pyodide
knitr::knit_engines$set(pyodide = function(options) {
    knitr:::one_string(c(
        "```{pyodide}",
        options$yaml.code,
        options$code,
        "```"
    ))
})
```

```{=html}
<style>
    .fragment.frag-bold.visible, .fragment_.frag-bold{
        font-size:1.1em;
        font-weight:900;
    }
  .fragment p::before, .fragment_ p::before{
  content: "•";
  font-size: 1.1em;
  padding-right: 0.5em;
  }
 
  .fragment p,  {
  margin-bottom: 1em !important;
  position: relative;
  padding-left: 0.5em;
  display: flex;
  align-items: first baseline;
  }
p:has(span.has-subbullet){
  margin-bottom: 0.5em !important;
}
    /* Custom fragment class */
 section .fragment.arrow-fragment-before {
  position: relative;
  padding-left: 1.5em; /* space reserved for the arrow */
  margin-left: -1.5em;
}

/* Arrow using ::before */
 section .fragment.arrow-fragment-before::before {
  content: "➤"; /* or use →, ▶, etc. */
  position: absolute;
  left: 0;
  /* top: 0; */
  opacity: 0; /* hidden until fragment is visible */
  transition: opacity 0.3s ease;
  top: 50%;
  transform: translateY(-50%);
}


/* When fragment becomes visible */
 section .fragment.arrow-fragment-before.visible::before {
  opacity: 1;
}

section .subbullet{
    margin-left: 1.3em;
    font-size:0.8em;
}

section div.subbullet p{
    margin-bottom: 1em !important;
}

section .subbullet.nobullet p::before{
    content: "";
}

  .section .centertitle{
    display: flex;
    justify-content: center;
    align-items: center;
    gap: 1em;
  }
  p:has(img)::before{
  content: "" !important;
}

div.faded-out{
    opacity: 0.5 !important;
}

</style>
```

## How to Collect and Analyze Scholarly Metadata (from scratch) with the Crossref REST API for absolute beginners
::: {.fragment_}
View this presentation online by going to: https://tinyurl.com/CNSodano-PYMS
:::

::: {.fragment_}
Reach out to me on discord or at cnsodano@gmail.com
:::
::: {.fragment_ .subbullet .nobullet}
Happy to help with any coding/research software engineering questions or struggles!
:::


## Who am I?
::::: {.columns}

:::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {.fragment .fade-in .subbullet fragment-index=2}

![](/images/PYMS_lunch_session/me_performing_TMS.jpeg){width="100%"}
<!-- Bachelor's Computer Science, subspecialty Neuroscience (EEG, TMS, tACS, sleep spindle research) -->
:::
::::

:::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {.fragment .fade-in .subbullet fragment-index=3}
![](/images/PYMS_lunch_session/tilburg_presentation_action_shot.jpeg){height="50%"}
:::

::: {.fragment .fade-in .subbullet fragment-index=4}
![](/images/PYMS_lunch_session/tilburg_presentation_screenshot.png){height="50%"}
:::
::::

:::::

<!-- ::: {.fragment .fade-in .subbullet fragment-index=5}
Feel free to reach out for coding help!
::: -->

<!-- 
::::: {.slide-content}
## What is this presentation? {.scrollable}


::: {.fragment .fade-in}
[A webpage! (not a static slide deck)]{.has-subbullet}
:::

::: {.fragment .fade-in .subbullet .nobullet}
https://tinyurl.com/CNSodano-PYMS
:::

::: {.fragment .fade-in}
Quarto + RevealJS (+ some other stuff) == interactive code!
:::

```{webr}
#| envir: webr_env
#| autorun: true
#| echo: false
library(listviewer)
display_msg <- function(){
    json = "{'pyms':{'is':'very cool'}}"
    listviewer::jsonedit(json)
}
```

```{=html}
<div class='fragment fade-in'>
```
```{webr}
#| envir: webr_env
print("Hello, world!")
#plot(1:20, (1:20)^2)
#display_msg()
```

```{=html}
</div>
```

<!-- - LOW: clickable altair plot here -->
<!-- ::::: --> 

<!--  -->

## What's Crossref? 

#### (and why do I care about their data?)

::: {.fragment .fade-in fragment-index=1}
::: {.fragment .semi-fade-out fragment-index=3}
[Not-for-profit member 'club' of over 24,000 research-work-producing organizations]{.has-subbullet}
:::
:::

::: {.fragment .fade-in .subbullet .nobullet fragment-index=2}
::: {.fragment .semi-fade-out fragment-index=3}
Academic publishers, universities, funders, museums, libraries, hospitals, ...
:::
:::

::: {.fragment .fade-in fragment-index=3}
::: {.fragment .semi-fade-out fragment-index=4}
Central location for the collection, storage, and enhancement of metadata for all research works produced by member orgs
:::
:::

## How Crossref collects metadata

![](/images/PYMS_lunch_session/all_diagrams/Slide0.PNG)

## How Crossref collects metadata

![](/images/PYMS_lunch_session/all_diagrams/Slide01.PNG)

## How Crossref collects metadata

![](/images/PYMS_lunch_session/all_diagrams/Slide1.PNG)

## How Crossref collects metadata

![](/images/PYMS_lunch_session/all_diagrams/Slide2.PNG)

## How Crossref collects metadata

![](/images/PYMS_lunch_session/all_diagrams/Slide3.PNG)

## How Crossref collects metadata

![](/images/PYMS_lunch_session/all_diagrams/Slide4.PNG)

## How Crossref collects metadata
![](/images/PYMS_lunch_session/all_diagrams/Slide5.PNG)


## What's Crossref? 

#### (and why do I care about their data?)

::: {.fragment_ .faded-out}
[Not-for-profit member 'club' of over 24,000 research-work-producing organizations]{.has-subbullet}
:::

::: {.fragment_ .faded-out .subbullet .nobullet}
Academic publishers, universities, funders, museums, libraries, hospitals, ...
:::

::: {.fragment_ .faded-out }
Central location for the collection, storage, and enhancement of metadata for all research works produced by member orgs
:::

::: {.fragment .fade-in }
::: {.fragment .custom .frag-bold}
[95% of all DOI clicks are Crossref-issued DOIs]{.has-subbullet}
:::
:::

::: {.fragment .fade-in .subbullet .nobullet }
Over 180 million records
:::

::: {.fragment .fade-in .subbullet .nobullet }
All free to access...
:::

<!--  -->
<!-- ## A motivating example: Antivaxx movement

![](/images/PYMS_lunch_session/lancet_article_with_retraction_notices_everywhere.png){.r-stretch fig-align="center"}

::: {.fragment .fade-in .subbullet .nobullet}
[DOI: https://doi.org/10.1016/s0140-6736(97)11096-0]{style="font-size:0.8em; opacity:0.8;"}
:::

:::{.notes}
- evidence of payments by lawyers
    https://www.thetimes.com/uk/science/article/mmr-doctor-given-legal-aid-thousands-00ftl80msbs
- evidence of stripped license by general medical council
    https://www.theguardian.com/society/2010/may/24/mmr-doctor-andrew-wakefield-struck-off
:::

:::: {.fragment .fade-in }
Question for you:

::: {.fragment .fade-in .subbullet}
Did the authors ever agree to retracting this claim of causal MMR Vaccine->Autism relationship?
:::

::::

::: {.fragment .fade-in }
Maybe Crossref knows?
:::

# A brief aside on "metadata requests" -->

## What is a "request"?

![](/images/PYMS_lunch_session/all_diagrams/Slide6.PNG)

## What is a "request"?

![](/images/PYMS_lunch_session/all_diagrams/Slide7.PNG)

## What is a "request"?

![](/images/PYMS_lunch_session/all_diagrams/Slide8.PNG)

## What is a "request"?
![](/images/PYMS_lunch_session/all_diagrams/Slide9.PNG)

## Making an API request
![](/images/PYMS_lunch_session/all_diagrams/Slide10.PNG)

## Making an API request
![](/images/PYMS_lunch_session/all_diagrams/Slide11.PNG)

## Making an API request
![](/images/PYMS_lunch_session/all_diagrams/Slide12.PNG)

<!--  -->

## An abridged metadata record
```{json filename="metadata.json"}
#| code-line-numbers: "||2-4|5|7,15|10-11,18-19|8"
#| echo: true
{
    "title": "RETRACTED: Ileal-lymphoid-nodular hyperplasia, non-specific colitis, and pervasive developmental disorder in children",
    "container-title": ["The Lancet"],           
    "DOI": "10.1016/s0140-6736(97)11096-0",                                                                                               
    "updated-by": [                              //<1>
        {                                        
            "type":"correction",                 //<2>
            "DOI": "10.1016/s0140-6736(04)15715-2",
            "source":"retraction-watch",         //<3>
            "updated":{                          
                "date-parts":[2004,3,6]          //<4>
            }
        },
        {
            "type":"retraction",
            "DOI": "10.1016/s0140-6736(10)60175-4",
            "source":"retraction-watch",
            "updated":{
                "date-parts":[2010,2,6]          //<5>
            }
        }
    ]                                           //<6>
}
```

---

![](/images/PYMS_lunch_session/Fulltext_Correction_Wakefield_et_al_1998.png){.nostretch fig-align="center" width="100%"}

---

![](/images/PYMS_lunch_session/Fulltext_Correction_Wakefield_et_al_1998_highlighted.png){.nostretch fig-align="center" width="100%"}

--- 

![](/images/PYMS_lunch_session/Fulltext_Correction_Wakefield_et_al_1998_highlighted_emphasis.png){.nostretch fig-align="center" width="100%"}


<!--  -->

## Information not even linked to on the original article webpage!

![](/images/PYMS_lunch_session/lancet_article_linked_articles.png){fig-align="center"}

<!--  -->

## Making an API request
![](/images/PYMS_lunch_session/all_diagrams/Slide12.PNG)

## Making **automated** API requests
![](/images/PYMS_lunch_session/all_diagrams/Slide13.PNG)

<!-- -->
## A bare-bones script example {.scrollable}

:::: {.panel-tabset .scrollable}
## R
```{r}
#| echo: false
library(reticulate)
library(listviewer)

display_json <- function(json){
    listviewer::jsonedit(json)
}
```

```{r}
#| echo: true
#| code-line-numbers: "||1-3|1-7|1-11|1-14|13-19||"
#| output-location: slide
# Imports
library(httr2) # To make the API request
library(glue)  # For concatenating string parts of request

# Constants
BASE_URL <- "https://api.crossref.org"
API_VERSION <- "v1"

# Specific to this request
endpoint <- "works"
identifier <- "10.1016/s0140-6736(97)11096-0"

# https://api.crossref.org/v1/works/10.1016/s0140-6736(97)11096-0
combined_request_url = glue("{BASE_URL}/{API_VERSION}/{endpoint}/{identifier}")

req <- request(combined_request_url) # Build a "request" object
response <- req |>
    req_perform() |> # Makes API request, returns response
    resp_body_json() # Extracts JSON data from response

display_json(response) # Custom function for tutorial, uses `listviewer` package
```

## Python
```{r}
library(listviewer)
library(reticulate)
library(jsonlite)
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
import json
def display_json(_):
    pass
```

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| code-line-numbers: "||1-2|1-6|1-10|1-13|12-17||"
# Imports
import requests  # To make the API request

# Constants
BASE_URL = "https://api.crossref.org"
API_VERSION = "v1"

# Specific to this request
endpoint = "works"
identifier = "10.1016/s0140-6736(97)11096-0"

# https://api.crossref.org/v1/works/10.1016/s0140-6736(97)11096-0
combined_request_url = f"{BASE_URL}/{API_VERSION}/{endpoint}/{identifier}"

req = requests.get(combined_request_url)  # Makes API request, returns response
response = req.json()  # Extracts JSON data from response

display_json(response)  # Custom function for tutorial, uses `jsonedit` javascript package
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
response_json_string = json.dumps(response) 
```

::: {.fragment}
```{r}
#| echo: false
listviewer::jsonedit(py$response_json_string)
``` 
:::

::::


## Live Code-along!

```{=html}
<script>
globalThis.webrFetch = async function(url, options = {}) {
  const res = await fetch(url, options);
  const text = await res.text();

  return {
    status: res.status,
    headers: Object.fromEntries(res.headers.entries()),
    body: text
  };
};
</script>
```

::: {.panel-tabset}
## R
```{webr}
#| envir: webr_env
#| autorun: true
#| echo: false
library(jsonlite)
library(listviewer)

display_json <- function(json_){
    listviewer::jsonedit(json_)
}
invisible(webr::eval_js("
globalThis.webrFetchSync = function(url) {
  const xhr = new XMLHttpRequest();

  xhr.open('GET', url, false);   // false = synchronous
  xhr.send(null);

  if (xhr.status >= 200 && xhr.status < 300) {
    return xhr.responseText;
  }

  throw new Error('HTTP ' + xhr.status + ': ' + xhr.statusText);
};

null
"))

fetch_webr <- function(url) {
  js <- sprintf(
    "globalThis.webrFetchSync('%s')",
    url
  )
  webr::eval_js(js)
}

req_perform_ <- function(req){
   return(fetch_webr(req$url))
}

resp_body_json_ <- function(resp){
    # No-op; required to make the tutorial syntax match the real R syntax, but because of limitations of webr the httr2 requests can't be made and so I replace with a wrapper for the javascript fetch api
    return(resp) 
}
```

```{webr}
#| envir: webr_env
# Imports
library(httr2)  # To make the API request
library(glue)   # For concatenating string parts of request

# Constants
BASE_URL <- "https://api.crossref.org"
API_VERSION <- "v1"

# Specific to this request
endpoint <- "works"
identifier <- "10.1016/s0140-6736(97)11096-0"

# https://api.crossref.org/v1/works/10.1016/s0140-6736(97)11096-0
combined_request_url = glue("{BASE_URL}/{API_VERSION}/{endpoint}/{identifier}")

req <- request(combined_request_url) # Build a "request" object
response <- req |>
    req_perform_() |> # Makes API request, returns response 
    resp_body_json_() # Extracts JSON data from response

display_json(response) # Custom function for tutorial, uses `listviewer` package
```

## Python
```{pyodide}
#| envir: pyowjsoneditor
#| autorun: true
#| echo: false
import json
from IPython.display import HTML, display
unique_jsoneditor_ct = 0
def display_json(json_):
    global unique_jsoneditor_ct
    unique_jsoneditor_ct += 1
    if (type(json_)==dict):
        json_ = json.dumps(json_)
    return display(HTML(f"""
    <div id="editor-id{unique_jsoneditor_ct}" style="height:400px"></div>
    <script type="module">
        import {{ createJSONEditor }} from "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/vanilla-jsoneditor@3.11.0/standalone.min.js"
        const JSON_tutorial_json_editor = createJSONEditor({{
            target: document.getElementById('editor-id{unique_jsoneditor_ct}'),
            props: {{
                content: null,
                onChange: (updatedContent, previousContent, {{ contentErrors, patchResult }}) => {{
                    // content is an object {{ json: unknown }} | {{ text: string }}
                    console.log('onChange', {{ updatedContent, previousContent, contentErrors, patchResult }})
                    content = updatedContent
                }}
            }}
        }})
        let initialJson = {json_}
        JSON_tutorial_json_editor.set({{"text":undefined, "json": initialJson}})
        document.getElementById("editor-id{unique_jsoneditor_ct}").style.height="60vh"
    </script>
    """))
```
```{pyodide}
#| envir: pyowjsoneditor
# Imports 
import requests # To make the API request

# Constants
BASE_URL = "https://api.crossref.org"
API_VERSION = "v1"

# Specific to this request
endpoint = "works"
identifier = "10.1016/s0140-6736(97)11096-0"

# https://api.crossref.org/v1/works/10.1016/s0140-6736(97)11096-0
combined_request_url = f"{BASE_URL}/{API_VERSION}/{endpoint}/{identifier}"

req = requests.get(combined_request_url) # Makes API request, returns response 
response = req.json() # Extracts JSON data from response

display_json(response) # Custom function for tutorial, uses `jsonedit` javascript package
```
:::

## What if I want more than one metadata record?

::: {.panel-tabset}
## R
```{webr}
#| envir: webr_env
# Imports
library(httr2)  # To make the API request
library(glue)   # For concatenating string parts of request

# Constants
BASE_URL <- "https://api.crossref.org"
API_VERSION <- "v1"

# Specific to this request
endpoint <- "journals" #! Notice change

# https://api.crossref.org/v1/works/10.1016/s0140-6736(97)11096-0
combined_request_url = glue("{BASE_URL}/{API_VERSION}/{endpoint}") #! Notice no more identifier

req <- request(combined_request_url) # Build a "request" object
response <- req |>
    req_url_query(query="russian law journal", rows="3") |> #! NEW
    req_perform_() |> # Makes API request, returns response 
    resp_body_json_() # Extracts JSON data from response

display_json(response) # Custom function for tutorial, uses `listviewer` package
```

## Python
```{pyodide}
#| envir: pyowjsoneditor
# Imports 
import requests # To make the API request

# Constants
BASE_URL = "https://api.crossref.org"
API_VERSION = "v1"

# Specific to this request
endpoint = "journals"

# https://api.crossref.org/v1/works/10.1016/s0140-6736(97)11096-0
combined_request_url = f"{BASE_URL}/{API_VERSION}/{endpoint}"

params = [("query", "russian law journal"), ("rows", "3")] #! NEW

req = requests.get(combined_request_url, params=params) # Makes API request, returns response 
response = req.json() # Extracts JSON data from response

display_json(response) # Custom function for tutorial, uses `jsonedit` javascript package
```
:::
